column1: # Example candidates.tsv — feed this to validate_links.py and apply_links.py
# Columns (tab-separated): slide<TAB>category<TAB>label<TAB>url<TAB>anchor<TAB>substring
#   slide      = 1-based PPTX slide number (matches slideN.xml)
#   category   = acronym | product | person | citation | standard
#   label      = display name on the References slide
#   url        = authoritative URL
#   anchor     = (optional) exact <a:t> content to match; if blank, auto-finds first run containing label
#   substring  = (optional) substring within anchor to hyperlink; defaults to label
# Lines starting with # are ignored. Blank lines OK.
# IMPORTANT — encoding the `anchor` column:
#   The anchor must match the slide's raw <a:t> bytes exactly. Different authoring
#   tools store dashes and quotes differently:
#     - Slides authored in Google Slides / many web tools: literal Unicode
#       characters (e.g., — — ' ).
#     - Slides authored in PowerPoint via certain templates: XML entities
#     - Ampersand is ALWAYS & in the XML, regardless of source tool.
#   To check which form your deck uses, grep the raw slide XML AFTER unpacking:
#     grep -c '–\|—' unpacked/ppt/slides/slide12.xml
#     # 0 = literal Unicode in this deck; nonzero = entities.
#   The rows below mix both forms to show the syntax for each.
# --- Simple cases: anchor == label, no need to specify ---
# --- Anchor + substring: link only "Inspire V" inside the title run ---
